methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Primoco UAV is a public-stage defense and security company founded by Ladislav Semetkovský, with €44M+ in order book backing. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 21 peers across public and private markets, yielding a comp universe of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. No same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers met the minimum threshold of three datapoints necessary for reliable triangulation within the Defense & Security vertical. Expanding to a full-sector median across all 21 peers would introduce heterogeneity bias—mixing drone manufacturers with broader defense contractors—and therefore was not pursued.

With the primary quantitative method constrained by data sparsity, this analysis cannot reliably support a point estimate or narrow valuation range. The €44M+ order book represents cash inflow visibility but does not directly translate to enterprise value without revenue recognition data, EBITDA margins, or growth multiples from sufficiently comparable peers. Further analysis would require either: (i) disclosed financial metrics from Primoco UAV enabling revenue or EBITDA multiples against public peers; or (ii) private financing rounds from the broader comp set providing implicit valuation signals. The current dataset is insufficient for defensible triangulation.
